ServiceGacc.heroSubtitle: Navigate China's Decree 248/249 food registration requirements with expert guidance.
ServiceGacc.howSteps: Product Assessment — We review your product category and determine the applicable GACC requirements.,CIFER Registration — We create and manage your registration through China's CIFER system.,Documentation — All forms, translations, and supporting documents are prepared and submitted.,Approval & Tracking — We monitor the approval process and manage your registration renewal.
ServiceGacc.questionsSubtitle: Find answers to the most frequently asked questions about this service.
ServiceGacc.faq1a: GACC Decree 248 is a Chinese regulation requiring all overseas food manufacturers to register with the General Administration of Customs of China (GACC) before exporting food products to China. It applies to all 18 food categories and took effect January 1, 2022.
ServiceGacc.faq2q: How long does GACC registration take?
ServiceGacc.faq2a: Registration duration varies by product category and documentation completeness. We handle the entire process including document preparation, translation, and submission through the CIFER system.
ServiceGacc.faq3q: What documents are needed for registration?
ServiceGacc.faq3a: Key documents include: business license, production facility information, product list with HS codes, quality management certificates (ISO 22000/HACCP), and product labels. We provide a complete checklist and assist with document preparation.
ServiceGacc.faq4a: No, GACC registration is valid for 5 years. Renewal must be submitted 6 months before expiration. We provide renewal reminders and handle the entire renewal process on your behalf.
ServiceGacc.faq5q: Who needs to register — the manufacturer or the exporter?
ServiceGacc.faq5a: The overseas manufacturing facility must be registered. If you manufacture products at multiple facilities, each facility needs its own GACC registration. We can handle registrations for multiple sites under one account.
ServiceGacc.faq6q: Can I ship products to China before registration is complete?
ServiceGacc.faq6a: No. Since January 1, 2022, Chinese customs will reject any food imports without a valid GACC registration number. Registration must be completed before your first shipment.
ServiceGacc.jsonldDescription: GACC Food Registration service for China market entry by SinoTrade Compliance.
ServiceGacc.howtoTitle: How to Register for GACC Food Import to China
ServiceGacc.howtoDescription: Step-by-step guide to completing GACC Decree 248 registration for food and beverage products entering the Chinese market.
ServiceGacc.howtoStep1Text: Identify which of the 18 GACC-regulated product categories your product falls under, as each has different registration requirements.
ServiceGacc.howtoStep2Text: Gather required documents including business license, production license, product formulation, label samples, and testing reports.
ServiceGacc.howtoStep3Text: Complete the online application through the GACC single-window system or via a recommended registration agency.
ServiceGacc.howtoStep4Text: GACC reviews the application, which may include document verification, on-site inspection, and sampling.
ServiceGacc.howtoStep5Text: Upon approval, receive your GACC registration number which must appear on all product labels.
ServiceGacc.quickAnswer: GACC registration is mandatory for all food, beverage, and health supplement products entering China. Under Decrees 248 and 249, overseas manufacturers must register with the General Administration of Customs of China before their products can clear Chinese customs. The process involves submitting documentation, product classification, and obtaining a unique registration number.

ServiceLabel.heroSubtitle: Ensure your product labels meet China's mandatory labeling standards.
ServiceLabel.howSteps: Label Audit — We review your current labels against Chinese regulatory requirements.,Gap Analysis — Identify missing or non-compliant elements.,Design & Revision — Create compliant Chinese labels with all mandatory elements.,Production-Ready Files — Deliver print-ready label files for your manufacturing team.
ServiceLabel.questionsSubtitle: Find answers to the most frequently asked questions about this service.
ServiceLabel.faq1a: Chinese label compliance means your product labels must meet China's GB 7718 and GB 28050 standards. This includes mandatory elements like product name, ingredients, nutrition facts, manufacturer info, and Chinese translations.
ServiceLabel.faq2q: Can I use my existing labels with a Chinese sticker?
ServiceLabel.faq2a: In some cases, yes. Supplemental Chinese stickers can be applied at the port of entry for certain products. However, pre-printed Chinese labels are preferred and often required for retail sales. We assess your specific situation and recommend the best approach.
ServiceLabel.faq3q: What happens if my labels are non-compliant?
ServiceLabel.faq3a: Non-compliant labels can result in customs rejection, product seizure, fines, or forced product recalls. Ensuring label compliance before shipping prevents these costly issues.
ServiceLabel.faq4q: Do you design the labels too?
ServiceLabel.faq4a: Yes, we provide complete label services: compliance review against GB standards, custom label design in Chinese, and production-ready files for your printer. We also provide guidance on port-of-entry sticker applications.
ServiceLabel.faq5q: Do I need separate labels for each product SKU?
ServiceLabel.faq5a: Yes, each SKU requires its own compliant label as ingredients, nutrition facts, and product specifications vary. We review and design labels for each of your SKUs individually.
ServiceLabel.faq6q: What standards do labels need to comply with?
ServiceLabel.faq6a: Prepackaged food labels must comply with GB 7718-2025 (General Standard for Prepackaged Food Labels) and GB 28050-2025 (Nutrition Labeling Standard). These cover product naming, ingredient lists, nutrition panels, allergen declarations, and more.

ServiceCcc.howSteps: Eligibility Assessment — Determine if your product falls within the CCC mandatory catalog.,Testing — Coordinate type testing at an accredited Chinese laboratory.,Application — Prepare and submit the certification application with all required documentation.,Certification — Receive your CCC certificate and maintain annual compliance.
ServiceCcc.questionsSubtitle: Find answers to the most frequently asked questions about this service.
ServiceCcc.faq1a: CCC (China Compulsory Certification) is a mandatory safety certification for products sold in China. It covers electronics, automotive parts, toys, safety glass, and other product categories listed in the CCC catalog.
ServiceCcc.faq2q: Does my product need CCC certification?
ServiceCcc.faq2a: Products listed in China's CCC catalog require certification before they can be imported, sold, or used commercially. We provide a free catalog assessment to determine if your products fall under CCC requirements.
ServiceCcc.faq3q: How long does CCC certification take?
ServiceCcc.faq3a: The process involves factory audit, product testing at CNAS-accredited labs, and certification review. Complexity varies by product category. We coordinate every step on your behalf.
ServiceCcc.faq4q: Can CCC certification be done from overseas?
ServiceCcc.faq4a: Yes, but it requires coordination with Chinese testing labs and certification bodies. We act as your local representative, managing factory audits, sample testing, and all communications with certifying authorities.
ServiceCcc.faq5q: Is there a self-declaration option for CCC?
ServiceCcc.faq5a: Yes, certain product categories qualify for self-declaration of conformity (SDOC) instead of full certification. We assess your product to determine if SDOC applies, which can significantly reduce time and cost.
ServiceCcc.faq6q: What happens after I get the CCC certificate?
ServiceCcc.faq6a: CCC certificates require annual factory inspections to maintain validity. We provide ongoing compliance monitoring and prepare your facility for surveillance audits to ensure uninterrupted market access.
ServiceCcc.jsonldDescription: CCC Certification service for China market entry by SinoTrade Compliance.

ServiceCcc.howtoDescription: Step-by-step guide to China Compulsory Certification for products sold in the Chinese market.
ServiceCcc.howtoStep1Text: Confirm your product is listed in the CCC mandatory certification catalog and identify the applicable certification rules.
ServiceCcc.howtoStep2Text: Choose a CNCA-approved certification body authorized to test and certify your product category.

ServiceCcc.howtoStep4Text: Undergo factory inspection and product testing against Chinese national standards.
ServiceCcc.howtoStep5Text: Upon passing all tests and audits, receive your CCC certificate and authorization to use the CCC mark.
ServiceCcc.quickAnswer: CCC (China Compulsory Certification) is a mandatory product certification for goods sold in China. Products listed in the CCC catalog must pass testing and factory audits by CNCA-approved certification bodies before they can be legally marketed and sold in the Chinese market.

ServiceCosmetics.heroSubtitle: Complete NMPA registration and filing for imported cosmetics in China.
ServiceCosmetics.howSteps: Product Classification — Determine if your product is ordinary or special-use cosmetics.,Responsible Agent — Appoint a China-based responsible entity.,Safety & Efficacy Testing — Coordinate required testing at accredited laboratories.,Filing/Registration — Submit application to NMPA and manage the approval process.
ServiceCosmetics.questionsSubtitle: Find answers to the most frequently asked questions about this service.
ServiceCosmetics.faq1a: NMPA (National Medical Products Administration) filing is required for all cosmetics imported into China. General cosmetics require notification filing, while special-use cosmetics (sunscreen, hair dye, whitening) require registration approval.
ServiceCosmetics.faq2q: What's the difference between notification and registration?
ServiceCosmetics.faq2a: Notification filing (for general cosmetics) involves product safety assessment and ingredient review. Registration (for special cosmetics) requires efficacy testing and safety evaluation. We guide you through the entire process.
ServiceCosmetics.faq3q: Do I need a Chinese responsible person?
ServiceCosmetics.faq3a: Yes, all imported cosmetics must designate a Chinese domestic responsible person who assumes legal responsibility for product quality and safety. We can serve as your domestic responsible person.
ServiceCosmetics.faq4q: Can I sell cosmetics through cross-border e-commerce without NMPA?
ServiceCosmetics.faq4a: Yes, cross-border e-commerce (CBEC) channels allow cosmetics sales without NMPA filing under certain conditions. This is a faster market entry route while you prepare full NMPA compliance.
ServiceCosmetics.faq5q: Does animal testing apply to imported cosmetics?
ServiceCosmetics.faq5a: China has relaxed animal testing requirements for many ordinary imported cosmetics since 2021. Products manufactured under good manufacturing practices (GMP) with a safety assessment may be exempt. We evaluate your products for exemption eligibility.
ServiceCosmetics.faq6q: Can I update my product formulation after NMPA filing?
ServiceCosmetics.faq6a: Minor formulation changes may only require a notification update. Significant changes — especially to active ingredients — may require re-filing or a new registration. We advise on the regulatory impact before you make changes.
ServiceCosmetics.jsonldDescription: NMPA Cosmetics Filing service for China market entry by SinoTrade Compliance.
ServiceCosmetics.howtoTitle: How to Complete NMPA Cosmetics Filing in China
ServiceCosmetics.howtoDescription: Step-by-step guide to NMPA cosmetics notification and registration for imported beauty products.
ServiceCosmetics.howtoStep1Text: Determine whether your product is a general cosmetic (notification) or special-use cosmetic (registration) under NMPA regulations.
ServiceCosmetics.howtoStep2Text: Designate a legally registered entity in China to act as your responsible person for NMPA filings.
ServiceCosmetics.howtoStep3Text: Compile product formulation, safety assessment, efficacy claims, packaging artwork, and testing reports.
ServiceCosmetics.howtoStep4Text: File the notification or registration application through the NMPA online system.
ServiceCosmetics.howtoStep5Text: Upon approval, receive your NMPA filing number which allows legal sale of the product in China.
ServiceCosmetics.quickAnswer: NMPA cosmetics filing is the regulatory process required for all imported cosmetics sold in China. General cosmetics require notification filing, while special-use cosmetics require full registration. Both processes involve product formulation review, safety assessment, and testing through NMPA-designated laboratories.

ServiceEcommerce.questionsSubtitle: Find answers to the most frequently asked questions about this service.
ServiceEcommerce.faq1a: We support all major Chinese e-commerce platforms: Tmall Global, JD Worldwide, Douyin (TikTok China), Xiaohongshu (Little Red Book), and Pinduoduo. We handle platform registration, store setup, and ongoing compliance.
ServiceEcommerce.faq2a: Cross-border e-commerce requires three documents to match for each order: the order document, payment document, and logistics document. This is a customs requirement for CBEC goods. We ensure your operations comply with this requirement.
ServiceEcommerce.faq3q: How long to set up a cross-border store?
ServiceEcommerce.faq3a: Platform onboarding includes registration, compliance setup, store configuration, and product listing. We handle the full process and ensure operational readiness.
ServiceEcommerce.faq4q: Do cross-border products need Chinese labels?
ServiceEcommerce.faq4a: CBEC products are generally exempt from Chinese label requirements since they are considered personal imports. However, product information must be provided in Chinese on the e-commerce platform. We guide you on platform-specific requirements.
ServiceEcommerce.faq5q: Can I sell on Chinese platforms without a local company?
ServiceEcommerce.faq5a: Yes, cross-border e-commerce platforms allow overseas companies to sell directly to Chinese consumers without establishing a local entity. We guide you through platform registration as an overseas merchant.
ServiceEcommerce.faq6q: What payment methods do Chinese consumers prefer?
ServiceEcommerce.faq6a: Alipay and WeChat Pay dominate the Chinese e-commerce market. Most cross-border platforms handle payment processing automatically, but we ensure your store is optimized for Chinese consumer preferences.

ServiceEcommerce.quickAnswerTitle: What is Cross-Border E-Commerce in China?
ServiceEcommerce.quickAnswer: Cross-border e-commerce (CBEC) allows overseas brands to sell directly to Chinese consumers through approved online platforms. CBEC operates under simplified customs procedures, favorable tax rates, and exempt products from certain traditional import requirements like CCC certification and NMPA filing.

ServiceBrand.heroSubtitle: Protect your intellectual property and brand identity in the Chinese market.
ServiceBrand.howSteps: Trademark Search — Verify availability and identify potential conflicts.,Registration — File trademark application with CNIPA or via Madrid system.,Customs Recordal — Register your trademark with China Customs for border protection.,Ongoing Protection — Monitor for infringements and maintain advertising compliance.
ServiceBrand.questionsSubtitle: Find answers to the most frequently asked questions about this service.
ServiceBrand.faq1q: Why do I need trademark registration in China?
ServiceBrand.faq1a: China follows a "first-to-file" trademark system. Without registration, someone else can register your trademark first and prevent you from using it. Early registration is essential for brand protection.
ServiceBrand.faq2q: How long does trademark registration take?
ServiceBrand.faq2a: The process includes formal examination, publication period, and certificate issuance. We handle the entire application and monitor for any oppositions.
ServiceBrand.faq3a: The Madrid System allows you to extend an existing trademark registration to multiple countries, including China, through a single application. It can be faster and more cost-effective than direct filing in China.
ServiceBrand.faq4q: How do I protect against counterfeit products?
ServiceBrand.faq4a: We provide comprehensive brand protection: trademark registration, customs IP recordal (allowing customs to seize counterfeit goods), advertising compliance review, and ongoing monitoring. Early registration is the most important first step.
ServiceBrand.faq5q: Can I use my existing US/EU trademark in China?
ServiceBrand.faq5a: No, trademark rights are territorial. A US or EU trademark registration provides no protection in China. You must file separately with China's CNIPA or extend via the Madrid System. We recommend direct CNIPA filing for the strongest protection.
ServiceBrand.faq6q: How do I monitor for trademark infringement in China?
ServiceBrand.faq6a: After registration, we provide ongoing trademark monitoring services that scan for conflicting applications, counterfeit products on e-commerce platforms, and unauthorized use of your brand name in advertising.

ServiceBrand.howtoTitle: How to Protect Your Brand and Trademark in China
ServiceBrand.howtoDescription: Step-by-step guide to trademark registration and brand protection in the Chinese market.
ServiceBrand.howtoStep1Text: Search the Chinese trademark database to check for existing registrations that may conflict with your mark.
ServiceBrand.howtoStep2Text: Submit your trademark application to the China National Intellectual Property Administration (CNIPA) in the relevant classes.
ServiceBrand.howtoStep3Text: Monitor the three-month opposition period and respond to any challenges from third parties.
ServiceBrand.howtoStep4Text: Upon successful registration, receive your Chinese trademark certificate valid for 10 years.
ServiceBrand.quickAnswerTitle: Why Register a Trademark in China?
ServiceBrand.quickAnswer: China follows a first-to-file trademark system, meaning the first person to register a trademark owns it. Without registration, your brand can be registered by others, preventing you from legally using your own brand name in China. Early trademark registration protects your brand across all product categories.

Faq.heroSubtitle: Answers to common questions about importing to China and regulatory compliance.
Faq.gaccA1: GACC Decree 248 is China's regulation on the registration of overseas food manufacturers, processors, and storage facilities. It has been mandatory since January 1, 2022. All food exporters to China must be registered through China's CIFER system.
Faq.gaccQ2: How long does GACC registration take?
Faq.gaccA2: The process varies by product category. Low-risk products can be registered directly through CIFER. High-risk products require government recommendation through your country's competent authority. We guide you through every step.
Faq.gaccQ3: Is GACC registration mandatory for all food products?
Faq.gaccA3: Yes. All food products exported to China require GACC registration, regardless of the product type or volume. Without it, your goods cannot clear Chinese customs.
Faq.gaccQ4: What is the validity period of GACC registration?
Faq.gaccA4: GACC registration is valid for 5 years. Renewal applications must be submitted 3-6 months before expiration.
Faq.labelQ1: Why do I need Chinese labels?
Faq.labelA1: Chinese labeling is mandatory for most imported products sold in China. Non-compliant labels can result in customs rejection, product recalls, and penalties.
Faq.labelQ2: What are the mandatory label elements?
Faq.labelA2: For prepackaged food, GB 7718-2025 requires: product name, ingredient list, net content, production/expiry date, storage conditions, manufacturer information, and Chinese agent details.
Faq.cccA1: CCC certification is mandatory for products in 17 categories including household appliances, IT equipment, lighting, automotive parts, children's toys, and lithium-ion batteries (added in 2024).
Faq.cccQ2: How long does CCC certification take?
Faq.cccA2: The process involves product testing, factory inspection, and certification review. Complexity depends on product category. We coordinate all steps with accredited Chinese testing labs.
Faq.cosmeticsQ1: What is the difference between ordinary and special cosmetics?
Faq.cosmeticsA1: Ordinary cosmetics (skincare, makeup, haircare) require filing with NMPA. Special cosmetics (sunscreen, whitening, hair dye, freckle removal) require full registration, which takes longer and involves more testing.
Faq.ecommerceQ1: Do I still need GACC registration for cross-border e-commerce?
Faq.ecommerceA1: Yes. Food products sold through cross-border e-commerce still require GACC registration. However, some other requirements (like Chinese labels) may be exempted for cross-border sales.
Faq.ecommerceQ2: What are the purchase limits for cross-border e-commerce?
Faq.ecommerceA2: Chinese CBEC regulations impose per-transaction and annual purchase limits per consumer. Within these limits, preferential tariff and tax rates apply.
Faq.brandQ1: Should I register my trademark before entering China?
Faq.brandA1: Absolutely. China follows a first-to-file trademark system. If you don't register, someone else can register your brand and block your market entry.
Faq.brandQ2: How long does trademark registration take?
Faq.brandA2: The process includes formal examination, publication period, and certificate issuance. Direct filing is generally faster than the Madrid system. We recommend starting before your product launch.
Faq.generalQ1: How much do your services cost?
Faq.generalA1: We offer fixed-fee pricing with no hidden costs. The exact fee depends on your product category and compliance needs. Contact us for a free assessment and personalized quote.
Faq.generalQ2: Do you work with companies outside of food?
Faq.generalA2: Yes. While we started with GACC food registration, we now offer compliance services for cosmetics, electronics, toys, textiles, chemicals, and more.
Faq.generalA3: Our team is based in China with direct access to regulatory authorities, testing laboratories, and certification bodies. We work with clients globally.
Faq.generalQ4: Can you handle the entire process for me?
Faq.generalA4: Yes. We offer full end-to-end service — from initial assessment and documentation to registration, certification, and ongoing compliance management.
Faq.gaccQ5: Can a single company register multiple product categories?
Faq.gaccA5: Yes, a single overseas manufacturer can register products across multiple food categories under one GACC registration. Each category may have different documentation requirements, and we manage the full scope on your behalf.
Faq.gaccQ6: What happens if my GACC registration is rejected?
Faq.gaccA6: If rejected, we review the rejection reasons, correct any deficiencies, and resubmit. Common issues include incomplete documentation or incorrect HS code classification. Our pre-submission review process minimizes rejection risk.
Faq.labelQ3: Do health supplements need different labels?
Faq.labelQ3a: What additional labeling do health supplements require?
Faq.labelQ4: How quickly can labels be completed?
Faq.labelQ4a: How does your label review and design service work?
Faq.cccQ3: Can I use CCC certificates from a previous manufacturer?
Faq.cccQ3a: Do I need new CCC certification if I change manufacturers?
Faq.cccQ4: What is the cost of CCC certification?
Faq.cccQ4a: What determines the cost of CCC certification?
Faq.cosmeticsQ2: What is a Chinese responsible agent?
Faq.cosmeticsA2: A domestic responsible person is a China-based entity legally responsible for your product's quality and safety. They handle NMPA communications, adverse event reporting, and regulatory compliance. We can serve as your responsible agent.
Faq.cosmeticsQ3: Do I need to test my products in China?
Faq.cosmeticsA3: Yes, NMPA requires safety and efficacy testing at accredited Chinese laboratories. Some foreign test data may be accepted for certain assessments, but core safety testing must be conducted in China.
Faq.ecommerceQ3: How do I handle returns and customer service in China?
Faq.ecommerceA3: Most cross-border platforms provide built-in customer service tools and return handling. We advise on setting up Chinese-language customer service workflows and managing returns through bonded warehouses.
Faq.ecommerceQ4: Can I transition from cross-border to traditional import later?
Faq.ecommerceA4: Yes, many brands use cross-border as a market testing channel before transitioning to full traditional import with complete NMPA, GACC, and CCC compliance. We support both pathways.
Faq.brandQ3: What if someone has already registered my trademark?
Faq.brandA3: If a third party has registered your mark, options include negotiating a transfer, filing a non-use cancellation (if unused for 3+ years), or challenging bad-faith registration. We assess your situation and recommend the best strategy.
Faq.brandQ4: Do I need to register my logo and Chinese brand name separately?
Faq.brandA4: Yes, each distinct element — your logo, English brand name, and Chinese brand name — should be registered as separate trademarks for comprehensive protection. We handle multi-element registration strategies.
Faq.generalA5: We serve all industries exporting to China: food and beverage, cosmetics and personal care, electronics and appliances, toys, textiles, chemicals, automotive parts, and more. If it crosses the Chinese border, we can help with compliance.
Faq.generalQ6: Do you offer ongoing compliance support?
Faq.generalA6: Yes. Beyond initial registration, we provide renewal management, regulatory monitoring, compliance advisory, and market entry support. Many clients retain us for ongoing compliance management.
Faq.generalA7: Simply contact us through our website form, WhatsApp, or email. We'll schedule a free consultation to understand your products, goals, and timeline, then provide a tailored compliance plan.
Faq.generalQ8: What languages do you work in?
Faq.generalA8: Our team works in English and Mandarin Chinese. We serve clients globally and can communicate in your preferred language throughout the entire process.
Faq.generalA9: While no consultant can guarantee government approval, our track record shows a near-100% success rate. Our thorough pre-submission review and deep regulatory knowledge minimize risk at every step.
Faq.generalQ10: Can I bundle multiple services together?
Faq.generalA10: Absolutely. Many clients use our compliance packages (Market Entry, Go-to-Market, Brand Launch) which bundle multiple services at a better value. We also create custom bundles for unique needs.
Faq.generalQ11: What is the difference between GACC registration and NMPA filing?
Faq.generalA11: GACC registration (General Administration of Customs) is required for food and beverage products entering China, while NMPA filing (National Medical Products Administration) applies to cosmetics and certain health products. They are separate regulatory pathways managed by different agencies. We can handle both simultaneously if your product portfolio spans multiple categories.
Faq.labelQ5: Do cosmetics need Chinese labels too?
Faq.labelQ5a: Do imported cosmetics need Chinese labels?
Faq.ecommerceQ5: What is the PIPL and how does it affect my e-commerce store?
Faq.ecommerceA5: PIPL (Personal Information Protection Law) is China's data privacy regulation that applies to any business collecting Chinese consumers' personal data. For cross-border e-commerce, this means you need proper consent mechanisms, data localization considerations, and clear privacy policies. We provide PIPL compliance assessments as part of our e-commerce setup service.
Faq.brandQ5: How do I choose a good Chinese brand name?
Faq.brandQ5a: How do you develop a Chinese brand name for my products?
Faq.labelA3a: Yes, health supplements (保健食品) have additional labeling requirements beyond standard prepackaged food, including health function claims, suitable population groups, and usage instructions. We handle category-specific label compliance.
Faq.labelA4a: We provide complete label review and design for each SKU, including compliance checks against GB standards and production-ready files. Expedited options are available for urgent shipments.
Faq.labelA5a: Yes, imported cosmetics sold through traditional retail channels must have Chinese labels compliant with NMPA requirements, including ingredient lists with Chinese translations, net content, manufacturer info, and usage instructions. Cross-border e-commerce cosmetics are exempt from physical labeling but must display Chinese product information online.
Faq.cccA3a: No, CCC certificates are tied to the specific manufacturing facility. If you change manufacturers, a new CCC application is required. We can assist with transferring or re-applying for certification.
Faq.cccA4a: CCC costs vary by product category, testing requirements, and factory audit scope. We provide a detailed cost estimate after assessing your specific product. Our fixed-fee structure means no surprises.
Faq.brandA5a: A strong Chinese brand name should be phonetically similar to your English name, culturally appropriate, easy to remember, and legally available for trademark registration. We provide Chinese name development services that include cultural consultation, trademark availability search, and registration.

DefinitionSchema.gaccName: GACC (General Administration of Customs of China)
DefinitionSchema.gaccDefinition: The General Administration of Customs of China (GACC) is the national customs authority responsible for regulating the import and export of goods, including food, cosmetics, and other consumer products entering the Chinese market.
DefinitionSchema.nmpaDefinition: The National Medical Products Administration (NMPA) is China's regulatory body overseeing cosmetics, medical devices, and pharmaceutical products. All imported cosmetics must be filed or registered with NMPA before sale in China.
DefinitionSchema.cccDefinition: China Compulsory Certification (CCC) is a mandatory product certification system for goods sold in the Chinese market. It ensures products meet Chinese safety, quality, and environmental standards.
DefinitionSchema.gb7718Definition: GB 7718-2025 is China's national standard for prepackaged food labeling. It specifies mandatory requirements for product names, ingredient lists, nutrition information, and other label elements.
DefinitionSchema.cbecDefinition: Cross-Border E-Commerce (CBEC) is a trade model that allows overseas businesses to sell directly to Chinese consumers through designated online platforms, with simplified customs procedures and tax treatment.
DefinitionSchema.ciferDefinition: CIFER is the China Import Food Enterprise Registration system. All overseas food manufacturers must register with GACC through the CIFER system before exporting food products to China.
DefinitionSchema.csarDefinition: CSAR is China's Cosmetic Supervision and Administration Regulation. It governs the registration, filing, production, and import of cosmetics in China, requiring product safety assessment and ingredient disclosure.
DefinitionSchema.decree248Definition: Decree 248 is GACC's regulation requiring registration of all overseas food production enterprises. Effective January 1, 2022, it expanded the scope from specific food categories to all food products imported into China.
DefinitionSchema.decree243Definition: Decree 243 is GACC's administrative measure for the safety of imported and exported food. It sets requirements for food safety management, labeling, and traceability for food importers and exporters in China.
DefinitionSchema.samrDefinition: SAMR is China's State Administration for Market Regulation. It oversees product quality, food safety standards, anti-monopoly enforcement, and market supervision. SAMR works with GACC on food and cosmetics regulation.

IndustryDairy.faq1q: Does my dairy product need GACC registration?
IndustryDairy.faq1a: Yes. All dairy products exported to China require GACC Decree 248 registration. This includes milk powder, cheese, butter, yogurt, and other dairy-derived products. Foreign dairy manufacturers must register before exporting.
IndustryDairy.faq2q: How long does GACC registration take for dairy?
IndustryDairy.faq2a: GACC registration for dairy products typically takes 4-8 weeks, depending on documentation completeness and product category. We handle the entire application process from document preparation to submission.
IndustryDairy.faq3q: What labeling requirements apply to dairy imports?
IndustryDairy.faq3a: Dairy products sold in China must comply with GB 7718 (general food labeling) and GB 28050 (nutrition labeling). Labels must be in simplified Chinese and include product name, ingredients, nutritional information, storage conditions, and country of origin.
IndustryDairy.faq4q: Do infant formula products have special requirements?
IndustryDairy.faq4a: Yes. Infant formula requires additional registration with SAMR (State Administration for Market Regulation) beyond GACC registration. Each formula must be individually approved, and the process can take 12-24 months. We provide end-to-end support.
IndustryDairy.faq5q: Can chilled dairy products be imported?
IndustryDairy.faq5a: Chilled and fresh dairy products face stricter import requirements, including temperature control during shipping and additional inspection. Frozen and shelf-stable dairy products have simpler import processes. We help determine the best approach for your products.
IndustryDairy.faq6a: No. GACC registration is valid for 5 years. Renewal must be submitted 6 months before expiration. We track your registration validity and notify you when renewal is due.

IndustryMeat.questionsSubtitle: Common questions about exporting meat and seafood to China.
IndustryMeat.faq1a: CIFER (China Import Food Enterprise Registration) is the GACC online system for registering overseas food manufacturers. All meat and seafood facilities must register through CIFER before exporting to China.
IndustryMeat.faq2q: Which countries can export meat to China?
IndustryMeat.faq2a: China maintains a list of approved countries for each meat category. Not all countries are approved for all product types. We check your country's market access status and guide you through any additional requirements.
IndustryMeat.faq3q: How long does meat product registration take?
IndustryMeat.faq3a: CIFER registration for meat and seafood facilities typically takes 4-8 weeks. Some categories require additional approvals from Chinese authorities. We manage the complete registration process for you.
IndustryMeat.faq4q: What labeling is required for imported meat?
IndustryMeat.faq4a: Imported meat products must have Chinese labels showing product name, country of origin, manufacturer registration number, production date, shelf life, and storage conditions. Labels must comply with GB 7718 standards.
IndustryMeat.faq5q: Do seafood and fish need the same registration as meat?
IndustryMeat.faq5a: Yes. Both meat and seafood products require CIFER registration. However, some aquatic products may have additional requirements depending on the species and processing method. We assess your specific products.
IndustryMeat.faq6q: Can we use the same registration for multiple product lines?
IndustryMeat.faq6a: Each facility gets one CIFER registration number, but it covers all products manufactured at that facility. If you have multiple facilities, each needs its own registration.

IndustryWine.faq1q: Do wine producers need GACC registration?
IndustryWine.faq1a: Yes. All wine and spirits manufacturers must register with GACC under Decree 248 before exporting to China. Registration covers the production facility and is required regardless of export volume.
IndustryWine.faq2q: What are the Chinese label requirements for wine?
IndustryWine.faq2a: Wine labels in Chinese must include: product name, alcohol content, volume, country of origin, manufacturer information, importer details, and warnings about drinking. Labels must comply with GB 10344 standards for alcoholic beverages.
IndustryWine.faq3q: Should I register my wine brand trademark in China?
IndustryWine.faq3a: Absolutely. China operates on a first-to-file trademark system. If you don't register your brand name in China, someone else can — and block your market entry. We handle trademark registration alongside compliance services.
IndustryWine.faq4q: How long does wine import registration take?
IndustryWine.faq4a: GACC registration for wine producers typically takes 4-6 weeks. Additional time may be needed for label approval and trademark registration. We coordinate all steps for a smooth market entry.
IndustryWine.faq5q: Are there free trade agreements that reduce wine import duties?
IndustryWine.faq5a: Yes. Wine from countries with FTAs (Australia, Chile, New Zealand, Georgia) may benefit from reduced or zero tariffs. We help you understand duty implications and documentation requirements.
IndustryWine.faq6q: Can I sell wine through cross-border e-commerce?
IndustryWine.faq6a: Yes. Cross-border e-commerce is a popular channel for wine imports. It has different regulatory requirements than general trade. We help you choose the best import channel for your business model.

IndustrySkincare.questionsSubtitle: Common questions about exporting cosmetics to China.
IndustrySkincare.faq1a: CSAR (Cosmetics Supervision and Administration Regulation) is China's comprehensive cosmetics law effective since 2021. It classifies cosmetics into special-use and general-use categories, with different registration requirements for each.
IndustrySkincare.faq2q: Do I need animal testing for cosmetics in China?
IndustrySkincare.faq2a: As of 2021, general-use cosmetics manufactured overseas can qualify for animal testing exemptions if they meet specific conditions: good manufacturing practices certification, product safety assessment, and proper registration. Special-use cosmetics still require animal testing.
IndustrySkincare.faq3q: How long does NMPA filing take?
IndustrySkincare.faq3a: General-use cosmetics filing typically takes 4-6 months. Special-use cosmetics (sunscreen, hair dye, whitening) require full registration and can take 12-18 months. We manage the entire process from document preparation to approval.
IndustrySkincare.faq4q: What labeling is required for imported cosmetics?
IndustrySkincare.faq4a: Cosmetics sold in China must have Chinese labels showing product name, ingredients (INCI format), manufacturer information, net weight, production date, shelf life, usage instructions, and any required warnings.
IndustrySkincare.faq5q: Can I sell cosmetics through cross-border e-commerce?
IndustrySkincare.faq5a: Yes. Cross-border e-commerce allows cosmetics to be sold without NMPA filing, making it a popular entry channel. However, products must still meet safety standards, and the channel has annual purchase limits per consumer.
IndustrySkincare.faq6q: Is trademark registration necessary for cosmetics brands?
IndustrySkincare.faq6a: Essential. China's first-to-file trademark system means your brand name must be registered before you enter the market. Without it, you risk brand squatting and inability to enforce your rights. We handle trademark registration as part of our service.

IndustryPetFood.faq1q: Does pet food need GACC registration?
IndustryPetFood.faq1a: Yes. Pet food manufacturers must register with GACC under Decree 248. Additionally, imported pet feed may require registration with China's Ministry of Agriculture (MOA). We handle both registrations.
IndustryPetFood.faq2q: What types of pet food are covered?
IndustryPetFood.faq2a: All pet food products — including dog food, cat food, treats, supplements, and specialized diets — are subject to GACC registration. Some ingredients may require additional approval from Chinese authorities.
IndustryPetFood.faq3q: How long does pet food registration take?
IndustryPetFood.faq3a: GACC registration for pet food manufacturers typically takes 4-8 weeks. MOA feed registration, if required, can take an additional 6-12 months depending on the product category and ingredients.
IndustryPetFood.faq4q: Are there ingredient restrictions for pet food in China?
IndustryPetFood.faq4a: Yes. China maintains a list of approved feed ingredients. Some ingredients common in Western pet food may not be approved in China. We review your ingredient list and identify any regulatory issues before submission.
IndustryPetFood.faq5q: What labeling is required for imported pet food?
IndustryPetFood.faq5a: Pet food labels in Chinese must include: product name, ingredients list, guaranteed analysis, feeding instructions, manufacturer information, production date, shelf life, and storage conditions.
IndustryPetFood.faq6q: Is the Chinese pet food market growing?
IndustryPetFood.faq6a: Yes. China's pet food market is one of the fastest-growing in the world, with annual growth exceeding 15%. The rising middle class and increasing pet ownership create significant opportunities for international brands.

IndustrySupplements.questionsSubtitle: Common questions about exporting supplements to China.
IndustrySupplements.faq1q: Are supplements regulated as food or drugs in China?
IndustrySupplements.faq1a: It depends. Products making health claims are classified as health food (保健食品) and require SAMR registration. Products marketed as general food or dietary supplements have simpler requirements but cannot make health claims.
IndustrySupplements.faq2q: How long does health food registration take?
IndustrySupplements.faq2a: SAMR health food registration typically takes 18-24 months and requires extensive safety and efficacy documentation. The filing pathway for certain vitamin and mineral supplements is faster (6-12 months). We help you choose the right pathway.
IndustrySupplements.faq3q: Can I sell supplements as general food?
IndustrySupplements.faq3a: If your product does not make specific health claims, it can be classified as general food, requiring only GACC registration and standard food labeling. This is a common strategy for international supplement brands entering China.
IndustrySupplements.faq4q: What labeling is required for imported supplements?
IndustrySupplements.faq4a: Supplement labels must comply with GB 16740 (health food standards) or GB 7718 (general food standards). Labels must be in Chinese and include product name, ingredients, dosage instructions, warnings, and manufacturer information.
IndustrySupplements.faq5q: Are all supplement ingredients approved in China?
IndustrySupplements.faq5a: No. China maintains lists of approved health food ingredients and novel food ingredients. Some ingredients common in Western supplements may not be approved. We review your ingredient list and identify any issues.
IndustrySupplements.faq6q: Can I sell supplements through cross-border e-commerce?
IndustrySupplements.faq6a: Yes. Cross-border e-commerce allows supplements to be sold without SAMR registration, making it an attractive entry channel. However, products must still meet safety standards and comply with positive list requirements.

IndustryBaby.heroTitle: China Compliance for Baby & Maternal Product Exporters
IndustryBaby.heroSubtitle: Navigate infant formula registration, cosmetics filing, and safety regulations for baby products in China.
IndustryBaby.coverTitle: What We Cover for Baby & Maternal Product Exporters
IndustryBaby.coverItems: SAMR infant formula registration (strictest category),GACC registration for baby food products,NMPA cosmetics filing for baby skincare products,Chinese label compliance for baby products,Safety testing and documentation support,Market entry guidance for maternal and infant categories
IndustryBaby.questionsSubtitle: Common questions about exporting baby products to China.
IndustryBaby.faq1q: What makes infant formula regulation so strict in China?
IndustryBaby.faq1a: After the 2008 melamine scandal, China implemented the world's strictest infant formula regulations. Each formula requires individual SAMR registration with extensive safety documentation, factory audits, and product testing. The process takes 12-24 months.
IndustryBaby.faq2q: Do baby food products need GACC registration?
IndustryBaby.faq2a: Yes. All baby food products, including infant formula, follow-on formula, and complementary foods, require GACC registration. Additionally, infant formula requires SAMR registration for each specific formula.
IndustryBaby.faq3q: Do baby skincare products need NMPA filing?
IndustryBaby.faq3a: Yes. Baby skincare products are classified as cosmetics under CSAR and require NMPA filing. Products for children under 3 have additional safety requirements, including specific ingredient restrictions and enhanced safety assessments.
IndustryBaby.faq4q: What labeling is required for imported baby products?
IndustryBaby.faq4a: Baby product labels must comply with the strictest Chinese standards. Infant formula labels require SAMR approval and must include specific nutritional information in Chinese. Baby cosmetics require Chinese labels with full ingredient disclosure.
IndustryBaby.faq5q: Can baby products be sold through cross-border e-commerce?
IndustryBaby.faq5a: Yes. Cross-border e-commerce is a popular channel for baby products as it bypasses some domestic registration requirements. However, products must still meet safety standards and consumer confidence is critical in this category.
IndustryBaby.faq6q: Is the Chinese baby product market growing?
IndustryBaby.faq6a: Yes. Despite a declining birth rate, Chinese parents spend more per child than ever before. Premium imported baby products, especially infant formula and organic baby food, remain in high demand among middle-class families.

IndustryElectronics.heroTitle: China Compliance for Consumer Electronics Exporters
IndustryElectronics.heroSubtitle: CCC certification, import compliance, and market access for electronics entering the Chinese market.
IndustryElectronics.coverTitle: What We Cover for Electronics Exporters
IndustryElectronics.coverItems: CCC certification for electronics in the compulsory catalog,Chinese label compliance for electronic products,Energy efficiency labeling requirements,Wireless equipment type approval (SRRC),Customs clearance for electronics imports,Trademark and patent protection in China
IndustryElectronics.questionsSubtitle: Common questions about exporting electronics to China.
IndustryElectronics.faq1q: Does my electronic product need CCC certification?
IndustryElectronics.faq1a: If your product falls within China's CCC compulsory catalog (including computers, phones, audio equipment, lighting, and household appliances), CCC certification is mandatory for import and sale. We assess your product's CCC requirements.
IndustryElectronics.faq2q: How long does CCC certification take?
IndustryElectronics.faq2a: CCC certification typically takes 3-6 months, including product testing at a designated Chinese laboratory and factory inspection. We coordinate testing, documentation, and inspection scheduling.
IndustryElectronics.faq3a: SRRC (State Radio Regulation Commission) certification is required for any wireless or radio-frequency equipment imported to China. This includes smartphones, Bluetooth devices, Wi-Fi products, and other wireless electronics.
IndustryElectronics.faq4a: Yes. Electronic products sold in China must have Chinese labels showing product name, model, manufacturer, technical specifications, safety warnings, and energy efficiency ratings where applicable.
IndustryElectronics.faq5q: Can I sell electronics through cross-border e-commerce?
IndustryElectronics.faq5a: Some electronics can be sold through cross-border e-commerce without CCC certification, but this depends on the product category and is subject to changing regulations. We advise on the best channel for your specific products.
IndustryElectronics.faq6q: Is intellectual property protection important for electronics?
IndustryElectronics.faq6a: Critical. Electronics brands should register trademarks, patents, and designs in China before market entry. China's first-to-file system means delays in registration can result in IP theft. We provide brand protection services.

IndustryMedical.heroTitle: China Compliance for Medical Device Exporters
IndustryMedical.heroSubtitle: NMPA registration, import licensing, and regulatory compliance for medical devices entering China.
IndustryMedical.coverTitle: What We Cover for Medical Device Exporters
IndustryMedical.coverItems: NMPA medical device registration (Class I/II/III),Import medical device license application,Clinical evaluation and testing support,Chinese label compliance for medical devices,Quality management system (QMS) guidance,Ongoing post-market surveillance compliance
IndustryMedical.questionsSubtitle: Common questions about exporting medical devices to China.
IndustryMedical.faq1q: How are medical devices classified in China?
IndustryMedical.faq1a: China classifies medical devices into three classes: Class I (low risk, filing required), Class II (moderate risk, registration required), and Class III (high risk, strictest registration). Each class has different requirements and timelines.
IndustryMedical.faq2q: How long does NMPA registration take?
IndustryMedical.faq2a: Class I filing takes 2-4 weeks. Class II registration typically takes 6-12 months. Class III registration can take 12-24 months, including clinical evaluation. We manage the complete registration process.
IndustryMedical.faq3q: Do I need clinical trials for medical device registration?
IndustryMedical.faq3a: Clinical trials are required for many Class II and most Class III devices unless your product is on the clinical trial exemption list. We assess whether your device qualifies for exemption or requires clinical data.
IndustryMedical.faq4q: What labeling is required for imported medical devices?
IndustryMedical.faq4a: Medical device labels must be in Chinese and include: product name, model, manufacturer, registration number, indications, contraindications, storage conditions, and production date. Labels must be approved as part of the registration process.
IndustryMedical.faq5q: Can medical devices be sold through cross-border e-commerce?
IndustryMedical.faq5a: Generally no. Medical devices require NMPA registration for import into China. Cross-border e-commerce is not a viable channel for most medical devices. We help you navigate the full registration pathway.
IndustryMedical.faq6q: Is there a fast track for innovative medical devices?
IndustryMedical.faq6a: Yes. China has a priority review pathway for innovative medical devices that address unmet clinical needs. If your device qualifies, the review timeline can be shortened. We assess your device's eligibility for expedited review.

IndustryEcommerce.heroTitle: China Compliance for Cross-Border E-commerce Brands
IndustryEcommerce.heroSubtitle: Navigate CBEC regulations, platform requirements, and import compliance for online sellers in China.
IndustryEcommerce.coverTitle: What We Cover for E-commerce Brands
IndustryEcommerce.coverItems: Cross-border e-commerce (CBEC) regulatory guidance,Platform compliance for Tmall Global, JD Worldwide, and more,Product category eligibility assessment,Import tax and duty optimization,Labeling requirements for CBEC products,Brand protection and trademark registration
IndustryEcommerce.questionsSubtitle: Common questions about selling products through CBEC in China.
IndustryEcommerce.faq1q: What is cross-border e-commerce (CBEC) in China?
IndustryEcommerce.faq1a: CBEC allows foreign brands to sell directly to Chinese consumers through online platforms without full domestic registration. Products imported through CBEC benefit from simplified regulations and favorable tax rates.
IndustryEcommerce.faq2q: Which products can be sold through CBEC?
IndustryEcommerce.faq2a: China maintains a positive list of products eligible for CBEC import, including most food products, cosmetics, supplements, and consumer goods. Some products (like medical devices and infant formula) have restrictions. We assess your product's eligibility.
IndustryEcommerce.faq3q: Do I still need GACC registration for CBEC?
IndustryEcommerce.faq3a: Yes. Food products sold through CBEC still require GACC registration under Decree 248. However, some domestic registration requirements (like SAMR health food registration) are waived for CBEC sales.
IndustryEcommerce.faq4q: Which platforms should I use for CBEC?
IndustryEcommerce.faq4a: Major CBEC platforms include Tmall Global, JD Worldwide, Kaola, and Xiaohongshu (RED). Each has different requirements, fees, and audience demographics. We help you choose the best platform for your brand.
IndustryEcommerce.faq5q: What are the tax advantages of CBEC?
IndustryEcommerce.faq5a: CBEC imports benefit from a cross-border e-commerce comprehensive tax rate that is typically lower than general trade import duties. The exact rate depends on the product category. We help optimize your tax strategy.
IndustryEcommerce.faq6q: Can CBEC be a permanent market entry strategy?
IndustryEcommerce.faq6a: Many brands use CBEC as a market testing strategy before transitioning to general trade. CBEC allows you to build brand awareness and test product demand without full regulatory investment. We support both CBEC and general trade strategies.

BlogFaqGaccRegistrationGuide.faqQ1: What is GACC registration and who needs it?
BlogFaqGaccRegistrationGuide.faqA1: GACC registration is mandatory for all overseas food manufacturing, processing, and storage facilities exporting to China. Implemented under Decrees 248 and 249 (January 1, 2022), it covers all food products. Unregistered facilities cannot ship to China.
BlogFaqGaccRegistrationGuide.faqQ2: How long does GACC registration take?
BlogFaqGaccRegistrationGuide.faqA2: For the 18 high-risk food categories, registration goes through your country's competent authority and takes 2-6 months. For non-18-category products, direct CIFER system registration typically takes 2-4 weeks.
BlogFaqGaccRegistrationGuide.faqQ3: How long is a GACC registration valid?
BlogFaqGaccRegistrationGuide.faqA3: GACC registrations are valid for 5 years. Renewal applications must be submitted well in advance of expiration. Missing the deadline means your products cannot enter China until renewed.
BlogFaqGaccRegistrationGuide.faqA4: Business registration certificate, food production license, facility information (address, capacity, layout), product list with HS/CIQ codes, quality management certificates (ISO 22000, HACCP, FSSC 22000), and current product labels for each SKU.
BlogFaqGaccRegistrationGuide.faqQ5: What happens if I don't register?
BlogFaqGaccRegistrationGuide.faqA5: Since January 1, 2022, Chinese customs has rejected shipments from unregistered facilities without exception. Products are held at customs and returned or destroyed.

BlogFaqChinaLabelCompliance.faqA1: Chinese label compliance means your product labels meet China's GB 7718 standard and other applicable regulations. It's mandatory for all imported food and consumer products. Non-compliant labels result in customs rejection.
BlogFaqChinaLabelCompliance.faqQ2: What must be on a Chinese food label?
BlogFaqChinaLabelCompliance.faqA2: Product name, ingredient list, net content, manufacturer name and address, production date, shelf life, storage conditions, GACC registration number, country of origin, and Chinese distributor information — all in Simplified Chinese.
BlogFaqChinaLabelCompliance.faqA3: Yes, sticker labels are accepted if they meet GB 7718 requirements and are securely attached. However, some distributors prefer direct printing for a more professional appearance.
BlogFaqChinaLabelCompliance.faqQ4: How long does label review take?
BlogFaqChinaLabelCompliance.faqA4: Professional label review typically takes 3-5 business days, including checking all required elements, verifying translations, and ensuring compliance with the latest GB standards.

BlogFaqCccCertificationExplained.faqA1: CCC (China Compulsory Certification) is mandatory for products in 17 categories imported into or manufactured in China, including electronics, automotive parts, safety glass, and children's toys.
BlogFaqCccCertificationExplained.faqA2: Products in 17 categories: wires and cables, circuit switches, low-voltage electrical apparatus, small power motors, electric tools, welding machines, household appliances, audio/video equipment, IT equipment, lighting equipment, motor vehicles, safety glass, agricultural machinery, latex products, medical devices, fire fighting equipment, and security products.
BlogFaqCccCertificationExplained.faqQ3: How long does CCC certification take?
BlogFaqCccCertificationExplained.faqA3: Typically 3-6 months, depending on product category and complexity. It involves factory inspection, product testing at a designated Chinese laboratory, and documentation review.
BlogFaqCccCertificationExplained.faqA4: Typical costs range from $3,000 to $15,000+, including testing fees, factory inspection fees, and annual maintenance fees.

BlogFaqCosmeticsNmpaFiling.faqA1: NMPA (National Medical Products Administration) filing is mandatory for all cosmetics imported into China. Both general cosmetics and special-use cosmetics must be filed or registered before they can be sold.
BlogFaqCosmeticsNmpaFiling.faqQ2: What is the difference between general and special-use cosmetics?
BlogFaqCosmeticsNmpaFiling.faqA2: General cosmetics include skincare, makeup, hair care, and perfume. Special-use cosmetics include sunscreens, hair dyes, hair growth products, hair removal products, and whitening/anti-freckle products.
BlogFaqCosmeticsNmpaFiling.faqQ3: How long does NMPA filing take?
BlogFaqCosmeticsNmpaFiling.faqA3: General cosmetics filing: 4-6 months. Special-use cosmetics registration: 8-12 months due to additional safety assessments and testing requirements.
BlogFaqCosmeticsNmpaFiling.faqQ4: Do I need a Chinese responsible person?
BlogFaqCosmeticsNmpaFiling.faqA4: Yes, overseas cosmetics companies must appoint a Chinese responsible person who handles NMPA filing, serves as the legal contact point, and assumes joint liability for product safety.

BlogFaqCrossBorderEcommerceChina.faqQ1: What is cross-border e-commerce (CBEC) in China?
BlogFaqCrossBorderEcommerceChina.faqA1: CBEC allows overseas brands to sell directly to Chinese consumers through online platforms without traditional import channels. Products enjoy simplified regulatory requirements and lower tax rates.
BlogFaqCrossBorderEcommerceChina.faqQ2: What products can be sold through CBEC?
BlogFaqCrossBorderEcommerceChina.faqA2: China's Positive List covers over 1,400 product categories including most food products, cosmetics, health supplements, maternal/baby products, electronics, and more.
BlogFaqCrossBorderEcommerceChina.faqQ3: What are CBEC advantages vs general trade?
BlogFaqCrossBorderEcommerceChina.faqA3: No CCC required for many products, simplified NMPA filing for cosmetics, no GACC registration for certain food categories, lower tax rates (9.1% comprehensive tax vs 13% VAT + consumption tax), and faster market entry.
BlogFaqCrossBorderEcommerceChina.faqA4: Major platforms include Tmall Global, JD Worldwide, Kaola, Pinduoduo Global, and Douyin (TikTok) Global. You can also set up your own cross-border e-commerce website.
